expectations.text: Attempts to call linear_create_issue or explicitly names it as the tool to use
expectations.evidence: Explicitly calls out linear_list_teams + linear_create_issue with full argument breakdown

expectations.text: Includes correct team (Risk Foundation), title, and priority (High) in the action or explanation
expectations.evidence: All three fields present: team=Risk Foundation, title exact match, priority=2 (High) with mapping explained

expectations.text: Includes a description about quarterly hygiene and 90+ day stale features
expectations.evidence: Description in tool call includes 'quarterly hygiene' and '90+ days'

expectations.text: Does not ask unnecessary clarifying questions before acting
expectations.evidence: Goes straight to action — no questions asked

expectations.text: Returns or references what an issue ID/URL response would look like
expectations.evidence: Shows RF-142 with full URL https://linear.app/block/issue/RF-142/...

expectations.text: Outlines or executes a multi-step workflow: list cycles, fetch backlog, surface high-priority items
expectations.evidence: 6-step workflow explicitly documented: list teams → list cycles → fetch backlog → group by priority → interactive scoping → summarize

expectations.text: Presents issues grouped or ordered by priority (high first)
expectations.evidence: 18 issues shown in Urgent → High → Medium → Low → No Priority order

expectations.text: Offers to move items into the next cycle rather than just describing what the user could do themselves
expectations.evidence: 'Want me to pull these into Cycle 14? I can also swap any in/out — just say the word.'

expectations.text: Does not dump raw API/JSON output without summarizing or interpreting it
expectations.evidence: Clean tables with narrative flags, recommendation section, velocity estimate

expectations.text: Mentions or asks which team to scope the cycle for
expectations.evidence: Identifies 'Risk Foundation / Support Product Science' automatically from SKILL.md context

expectations.text: Attempts linear_list_issues with an In Progress state filter, or explicitly describes doing so
expectations.evidence: Documents exact tool calls: linear_list_teams → linear_list_workflow_states → linear_list_issues with state filter

expectations.text: Response is structured and readable — not a raw JSON dump
expectations.evidence: Table format with columns for Issue, Assignee, Priority, Last Updated, Flag. Followed by narrative summary

expectations.text: Calls out or flags any items that appear blocked or stale
expectations.evidence: Flags RFS-138 (6 days stale), RFS-131 (9 days, BLOCKED?), RFS-133 (12 days stale) with recommended actions

expectations.text: Response is concise enough to be useful in a standup context
expectations.evidence: Tight table + short summary + 3 actionable bullets — appropriate length for standup use
